Artifact

elements.text:
Requirements

Identify following -
1. How does the user interact with the
feature / functionality.
2. What is the product behavior in response
to the user's action
3. **All the problematic actions users can
take and how product should gracefully
behave in response
-------------------------------------------

List all the -
1. decisions
2. tradeoffs
3. assumptions
4. scope IN & OUT
5. Risks - WHAT CAN GO WRONG?!
6. Constraints
7. Dependencies
8. Success metric - quality of experience

elements.text:
Requirements

Identify following -
1. How can we breakdown the behavior-
blueprint shared by previous step. 
2. Aim is to deliver the feature in
meaningful vertical chunks so that it can
be tested and reviewed properly at every
stage.

elements.text:
Requirements

Identify following -
1. Do we have source data or Are we going
to generate it
2. How does that data travel from origin to
the product surface
3. Which existing sub-systems CRUD & act
upon that data. What's the effect on the
data.
** This is a technical spec doc that
describes how the data should flow with
maybe a very high level of system design
view in mind (not implementation details
-------------------------------------------

List all the -
1. decisions
2. tradeoffs
3. assumptions
4. scope IN & OUT
5. Risks - WHAT CAN GO WRONG?!
6. Constraints
7. Dependencies

elements.text:
Requirements

1. Do a quick check to see if
the taxonomy doc is old; if so
refresh it with the `system-
taxonomy` skill and use that
skill to formulate a plan.
2. Plan in terms of broad
functionality i.e. system
taxonomy, like - routes,
agents, utilities, services,
asynchronous tasks, etc.
elements.originalText:
Requirements

1. Do a quick check to see if the taxonomy doc is old; if so refresh it with the `system-taxonomy` skill and use that skill to formulate a plan.
2. Plan in terms of broad functionality i.e. system taxonomy, like - routes, agents, utilities, services, asynchronous tasks, etc.

Agent Skill

System-Taxonomy (for consideration) +

elements.text:
Research

1. What is functionality is
already present that can be
used to implement the plan. 
2. Which functionality will be
impacted with the planned
changes
3. Find out answers to the
open questions.
4. We want to know the truths
about the codebase; features;
patterns and especially
everything that'll be touched
by the planned changes.
elements.originalText:
Research

1. What is functionality is already present that can be used to implement the plan. 
2. Which functionality will be impacted with the planned changes
3. Find out answers to the open questions.
4. We want to know the truths about the codebase; features; patterns and especially everything that'll be touched by the planned changes.
